title: Can automations specify a revoke URL?
excerpt:
When you build a board/item/custom view app, it’s a kinda free for all, so OAuth-based apps have to build all their screens for authorization. 
But when you build an automation, things are a lot more formal. You specify …

posts.author: dvdsmpsn (David @ David Simpson Apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
When you build a board/item/custom view app, it’s a kinda free for all, so OAuth-based apps have to build all their screens for authorization.

But when you build an automation, things are a lot more formal. You specify an authorization URL in the feature here:

Screenshot 2024-07-30 at 15.21.361170×1564 60.4 KB

That’s fine, but it misses out an equally important part, which is the revoke or “logout” URL so that after the automation has been removed or disabled, you should be able to tidy up and revoke any user tokens, or whatever.

Most automation apps I’ve seen on the marketplace have no way to revoke authorization, which means that there is an annoying extra step required to figure out how to revoke and work out where you’re even revoking authorization from.

This is not cool.

It should really be part of the feature screen for the automation.

If you have an auth URL, then a revoke URL should also be required (or at a push, optional).

For our Microsoft 365 Embedded app, we add a dropdown menu on all feature screens to allow users to log themselves out – deleting their user token from the data store:

Screenshot 2024-07-30 at 15.31.52816×520 71.7 KB

They then get the following confirmation modal:

Screenshot 2024-07-30 at 15.38.471334×460 31.3 KB

Aside: Microsoft also isn’t very good with revoke URLs for OAuth, so we have to jump through extra hoops, but we live in hope.

We use the same auth and revoke URLs for both view and automation features, its just that for view features, the auth has to jump out into a new window.

For admins, we also allow them to delete all user tokens for their account in the App Settings screen:

Screenshot 2024-07-30 at 15.32.322616×1818 390 KB

I’m not saying this is the best way – it’s the best we have come up with for now – but standardization of approach for all marketplace apps would be useful for end users.

I know that other platforms have a specific area where you can view and revoke all the connected apps for your user in the account.

Perhaps this would be useful?

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you @dvdsmpsn for the context and the request!

I have turned this into a request for our team to see
